query: ok we just spent 20 minutes discussing whether to use SQLite or Postgres for the local cache. Let's go with SQLite — it's simpler and we don't need multi-process writes. Can you make sure we don't lose that rationale?
I'm starting a new Go project for a CLI tool that processes log files. Let's set up the project structure and get institutional memory going from day one.
wait, why did we decide to use append-only IDs instead of renumbering? I feel like we discussed this last week but I can't remember the reasoning
the NOTES.md file is getting really unwieldy — there are like 15 sections and half of them have stuff that doesn't belong. Can we clean it up?
I told you three sessions ago that we should never use merge commits in this project. Can you note that as an invariant?
we just decided to drop the WebSocket approach and go with SSE instead. The main reasons were browser compatibility and simpler server implementation. Let's not forget this.
can you add a section to our notes about the naming conventions we've been using? I keep having to correct the agent on camelCase vs snake_case
I want to capture the user's exact words from earlier — they said 'the API should feel like a local function call, not a remote procedure'. That's a key design principle.
this project has notes files in three different subdirectories and I'm not sure which one to write to. The guide/ folder has its own notes but the API decisions keep ending up in the root file.
what decisions have we already made about authentication? I don't want to re-litigate something we already settled.
can you write a Python function that reads a CSV file and outputs the top 10 rows sorted by the 'revenue' column? The file is at data/q4_sales.csv
I need to fix this failing test in tests/test_auth.py — it's throwing a 401 on the refresh token endpoint. Here's the traceback...
can you review this pull request? It's PR #247 on our repo, adds rate limiting to the /api/v2/search endpoint
set up a new React component for the user settings page. It should have tabs for Profile, Notifications, and Security. Use our existing SettingsLayout component.
I need to write a README for this project explaining how to install it, configure the database, and run the test suite
can you add a CHANGELOG.md entry for version 2.3.0? We added SSE support, fixed the auth bug, and deprecated the old WebSocket endpoint
let's refactor the database module to use connection pooling. The current approach opens a new connection per request and it's killing performance under load
I want to set up git hooks that run linting and tests before every commit. We're using husky and lint-staged.
can you create a TODO list of everything we need to do before the v1.0 release? I want to track progress on the remaining features
help me debug this memory leak — the Node process keeps growing to 2GB after about an hour of running. I think it's in the event listener cleanup
